seoTitle: Duplicate a Form or Quiz in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Clone an existing Microsoft Forms survey or quiz in one click, rename the copy, and share a template link so colleagues can duplicate it too.
intro: Rebuilding a form from scratch every time you need a similar survey wastes time and risks copying mistakes. Microsoft Forms lets you duplicate an existing form internally or share it as a template for others, giving you a clean copy with zero prior responses attached.

steps.heading: Open your forms list
steps.body: Go to the My Forms tab to see all your existing survey and quiz tiles.

steps.heading: Copy the form
steps.body: Hover over the form you want to clone, click the three-dot More Options menu, and select Copy to create an instant duplicate.

steps.heading: Rename the new copy
steps.body: Open the freshly cloned form and edit the title field so it's clearly distinguished from the original.

steps.heading: Share as a template if needed
steps.body: For colleagues who need their own editable copy, use the Share as a template option under Collect Responses to generate a link they can duplicate into their own account.

steps.heading: Confirm the data is clean
steps.body: Check that the duplicated form starts with zero responses, since duplication copies structure only, never past submissions.

faq.question: Does duplicating a form copy the existing responses too?
faq.answer: No, a duplicated form always starts with a completely clean slate and no participant response data.

faq.question: Can I share a template with someone outside my organization?
faq.answer: The Share as a template link lets other users generate their own duplicate, subject to your organization's external sharing settings.

faq.question: Where does the duplicate appear after I copy a form?
faq.answer: It shows up immediately in your My Forms list, usually labeled with a numerical suffix until you rename it.

faq.question: Can I duplicate a quiz the same way as a regular form?
faq.answer: Yes, the Copy option works the same way for both quizzes and standard forms.
